content: We can reframe a memory and reflection module as an 'intrinsic curiosity module for long-term agent development', where reflection on existing stored memories acts as an internal reward signal that drives the agent to explore under-examined past experiences rather than just novel external environments. This creates a two-layer curiosity framework for 2025 autonomous agents: ICM drives exploration of new external states, while the memory-reflection module drives structured exploration of the agent's own accumulated knowledge.
